ticketTitle: Run the full test suite (go test ./... -count=1), fix any regressions in existing tests that assumed all languages produce full dimension sets or non-N/A grades, and ensure the build compiles cleanly.

deliverables.objective: Create internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go to lock in the analyzer-registry contract: ForLanguage returns nil for swift/unknown and non-nil for go/ts/js/py/rs; certifier yields N/A for unsupported-language units
deliverables.planText:
Create internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go with table-driven tests:
- TestForLanguage_Supported: verify ForLanguage returns non-nil for "go", "ts", "js", "py", "rs"
- TestForLanguage_Unsupported: verify ForLanguage returns nil for "swift" and "brainfuck"
- TestCertifier_UnsupportedLanguageYieldsNA: construct a minimal Unit with language "swift", certify it via engine.Certify (or the appropriate entry point), assert the resulting record has grade N/A and score 0.0
Import analysis package for ForLanguage, engine package for certifier. Use the project's existing test patterns (no external test fixtures beyond what the certifier already accepts).
nonGoals: Do NOT modify any production code in analysis/, engine/certifier.go, or engine/pipeline.go
Do NOT add new analyzer registrations
Do NOT introduce the optional Swift analysis tier
Do NOT touch cmd/certify/init_cmd.go or testdata/policies/

deliverables.objective: Create internal/report/report_test.go with N/A-grade rendering, unsupported-banner, and aggregate-exclusion test cases for the report layer
deliverables.planText:
Create internal/report/report_test.go covering the unsupported-language seam in reporting:
- TestCard_UnsupportedBanner: construct a Card (or use GenerateCard) with records that include unsupported-language units, verify UnsupportedCount > 0 and UnsupportedLanguages is populated, and that the formatted output includes an unsupported-languages banner section
- TestCard_NAAGradeRendering: verify that records with N/A grade render correctly (not as F), appearing in output with an 'N/A' designation
- TestCard_AnalyzableUnits: verify that AnalyzableUnits excludes unsupported-language records
- TestFull_BuildLanguageDetailExcludesUnsupported: construct records mixing go (supported) and swift (unsupported), pass through buildLanguageDetail (or the Full report path), verify unsupported languages do not contribute to LanguageDetail aggregates or DimensionAverages
- TestFull_AggregateScoreExcludesUnsupported: verify overall aggregate score/grades are computed only from supported-language records
Use the project's existing test patterns and helper functions. Import internal/report and internal/engine packages as needed.
nonGoals: Do NOT modify internal/report/card.go or internal/report/full.go
Do NOT add test cases to card_test.go or full_test.go — this consolidates into report_test.go
Do NOT test production code paths that are not yet wired
Do NOT introduce new report formats or features

deliverables.objective: Verify internal/engine/scorer_test.go has no regressions — run existing tests, confirm they pass, and add a smoke test ensuring scorer handles N/A-grade records without panicking
deliverables.planText: Run existing scorer tests: go test ./internal/engine/ -run TestScorer -count=1. Confirm all pass (they only use Go evidence, no Swift/unsupported assumptions). Optionally add one smoke test: TestScorer_NAAGradeIsNoOp — pass a record with N/A grade through the scorer, verify it does not panic and the record is unchanged (scorer should be a no-op for N/A grades, or at minimum not crash). This is a verification deliverable; if all existing tests pass cleanly, no code changes are required.
nonGoals: Do NOT rewrite or refactor existing scorer tests
Do NOT add Swift-specific scorer test cases
Do NOT modify scorer production code
Do NOT introduce new scoring dimensions or policies
